events.title: Kerala Blasters FC vs. North East United FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 15, 2026 between Kerala Blasters FC and North East United FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tight odds in the Kerala Blasters FC vs. NorthEast United FC ISL clash reflect evenly matched firepower, with a draw edged at 51.5% amid 50.5% implied probabilities for each win, signaling trader caution on decisive outcomes. Kerala Blasters leverage strong home form at Kochi's Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium—unbeaten in four league games there this season—but face a resilient NorthEast United side riding a three-match unbeaten streak, including gritty away draws. No major injuries reported on official lists, yet Kerala's inconsistent finishing clashes with NEUFC's compact defense, echoing their head-to-head history of frequent stalemates (three draws in last five). Recent momentum keeps this midfield battle unpredictable, amplifying draw appeal.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

question: Elder Scrolls VI released by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Elder Scrolls VI is officially released in the US by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source will be official information from Bethesda Softworks or its parent company, Microsoft.

eventMetadata.context_description: The absence of any release window for Elder Scrolls VI, coupled with Xbox head Phil Spencer's recent confirmation that it won't ship before 2026, anchors the 82% market-implied probability on "No" by year-end. Bethesda's June 2024 Xbox showcase delivered a promising trailer highlighting early development progress in Skyrim's homeland, but historical patterns—Starfield's five-year announce-to-release cycle and ongoing Starfield/Fallout support—signal multi-year timelines for AAA RPGs. Trader consensus reflects realistic caution amid fan hype, with no catalysts like Game Awards hints expected to shift odds dramatically before December 31 resolution.

question: Will Starmer say "Mr Speaker" 20+ times during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for Keir Starmer's remarks at the next Prime Minister's Questions on November 13 hinges on persistent backlash to Labour's October 30 budget, particularly cuts to winter fuel payments affecting millions of pensioners, prompting Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch's pointed attacks. Recent PMQs exchanges highlighted fiscal austerity defenses, NHS waiting lists, and two-child benefit cap criticisms, with Starmer emphasizing economic stabilization needs. Polling shows Labour approval dipping amid donor gift scrutiny, amplifying scrutiny on any policy concessions. Traders price in cautious rhetoric avoiding U-turns, tracking pre-session leaks or backbench rebellions that could shift implied probabilities before the Wednesday noon Commons session.

question: Daniel Noboa out as President of Ecuador by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Daniel Noboa ceases to be President of Ecuador for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (Ecuador Time, GMT-5). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Daniel Noboa's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Daniel Noboa and the government of Ecuador; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Daniel Noboa's firm grip on power, solidified by a decisive April 2024 referendum victory where voters overwhelmingly approved tougher anti-crime measures amid Ecuador's narcoterrorism surge, anchors trader consensus at 83.5% against his removal by June 30. Official actions like declaring an "internal armed conflict" in January and deploying military to prisons have stabilized security without triggering constitutional crises or impeachment threats. No verified legal challenges, resignations, or coup attempts have emerged from primary sources, while Noboa's approval ratings hover above 50% on security handling. Upcoming legislative sessions pose routine hurdles, but historical precedents favor incumbent continuity absent major scandals, justifying low odds on early ouster.

question: Will "Ready or Not 2: Here I Come" score at least 73 on the Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the displayed Rotten Tomatoes “All Critics” Tomatometer score for Ready or Not 2: Here I Come (2026) is at least equal to the specified number at 10:00 AM ET on March 23, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If, for any reason, the resolution data is unavailable at this market's specified end time, the resolution source will be checked until the relevant data is available. This market will resolve to “No” if no data is available by March 27, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

question: Will Trump be impeached before his term ends?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes“ if the US House of Representatives, by simple majority vote, approves or passes one or more articles of impeachment of President Donald Trump between market creation and January 20, 2029, at 12:00 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Neither trial nor conviction by the US Senate, nor removal from office, is necessary to resolve this market to “Yes“.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the federal government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting will be used.

Trader sentiment weighs GOP loyalty against potential scandals from cabinet picks or foreign policy moves. A major bipartisan uproar could tip odds higher toward Yes, while unified Republicans or lack of impeachable offenses would drive them lower, with 2026 midterms as a key pivot.

question: Mariano vs Britney: Higher winnings at Hustler Casino on Friday?
description:
This market will resolve to "Mariano" if between Mariano and Britney, Mariano is the player with the higher net winnings at the end of the Hustler Casino's High Stakes Friday game scheduled for March 20, 2026.

This market will resolve to "Britney" if between Mariano and Britney, Britney is the player with the higher net winnings at the end of the game.

If either player does not participate at all in the Hustler Casino game, this market will resolve to the other player.

question: Aliens.gov confirmed immigration website?
description:
On March 18, 2026, rumors emerged that the U.S. federal government had registered the domains “aliens.gov” and "alien.gov."

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the U.S. government officially announces or confirms that “aliens.gov” or "alien.gov" is intended for immigration-related purposes, or if the website becomes publicly accessible and its content is clearly and predominantly related to U.S. immigration or information for or about non-citizens (“aliens”), by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For announcements to qualify, they must be official. For confirmations to qualify, they must be unambiguous, on-the-record public statements issued by an authorized governmental person or entity, speaking in an official capacity.

If either website becomes publicly accessible and its content is clearly and predominantly related to extraterrestrial/UFO topics or any subject not primarily related to immigration, or if any qualifying announcement or confirmation establishes that the domains are intended for such non-immigration-related purposes, this market will resolve to "No".

Informal statements, placeholder pages, inactive domains, or any content that is not clearly indicative of the domains' primary purpose will not be considered.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed contestant is in the final 3 players of Big Brother Brasil 26.

If Big Brother Brasil 26 concludes without a winner being declared, or if Big Brother Brasil 26 has otherwise not concluded by May 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET this market will resolve to "No". In the case of a tie for any relevant ranking, this market will resolve to the names of listed contestants involved in the tie. 

This market will remain open until the conclusion of the season.

The resolution source for this market will be the official broadcast of Big Brother Brasil 26.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors early fan favorites like rumored housemate Giovanna and returning legends from prior seasons, with implied probabilities hovering around 40% for her top-3 finish amid scarce confirmed casting news for Big Brother Brasil 26, set to premiere in early 2026 on Globo. Recent developments include teaser announcements sparking social media buzz, but no official lineup yet, leaving odds volatile and tied to pre-season hype cycles seen in past BBB editions where underdogs surged via viral moments. Key dynamics: Brazil's reality TV audience loyalty to charismatic personalities and strategic gameplay, with eviction immunity twists often propelling alliances. Watch for December 2025 cast reveals, which could swing markets as historical precedents show 30-50% probability shifts post-announcement.

question: Game Handicap: XYG (-1.5) vs XQG Esports Club (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Honor of Kings match between Xianyou Gaming and XQG Esports Club in the KPL Growth League Group Stage, initially scheduled for March 21 at 1:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Xianyou Gaming" if Xianyou Gaming wins 2 or more games than XQG Esports Club in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "XQG Esports Club". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/honorofkings/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/honorofkings/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: XYG (-1.5) vs XQG Esports Club (+1.5)

events.title: Honor of Kings: Xianyou Gaming vs XQG Esports Club (BO5) - KPL Growth League Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Honor of Kings match between Xianyou Gaming and XQG Esports Club in the KPL Growth League Group Stage, initially scheduled for March 21 at 1:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Xianyou Gaming" if Xianyou Gaming win the match against XQG Esports Club.

This market will resolve to "XQG Esports Club" if XQG Esports Club win the match against Xianyou Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/honorofkings/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/honorofkings/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xianyou Gaming holds a slight edge in trader consensus for their BO5 matchup against XQG Esports Club in the KPL Growth League group stage, driven by their stronger recent form with three straight wins, including a dominant 3-1 over a top contender last week. XQG, meanwhile, snapped a two-match skid but struggles on maps favoring aggressive early-game strategies where Xianyou excels, per head-to-head history showing Xianyou's 4-2 record this season. No key roster changes or bans reported, though XQG's midlaner showed fatigue in recent scrim leaks. Rest advantage tilts to Xianyou post-bye week, but XQG's home crowd could spark an upset in this mid-table clash with playoff seeding implications.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors FURIA at around 70% implied probability to win this BO3 Group A clash at BLAST Open Rotterdam, driven by their superior recent form and firepower on the server. FURIA enters on a three-match win streak across tier-1 events, bolstered by consistent performances from riflers KSCERATO and FNC, while Aurora Gaming struggles with a 1-4 record in recent BO3s, hampered by inconsistent CT-side execution and key losses in clutches. No roster changes reported for either side per official HLTV updates; head-to-head favors FURIA 2-0 this year. Watch for map vetoes—FURIA dominates Mirage and Nuke, Aurora prefers Ancient—amid high stakes for group advancement.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 20, 10PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Will Charles Hittler be the next mayor of Arcis-sur-Aube?
description:
The second round municipal election to elect the municipal council of Arcis-sur-Aube is scheduled to take place on March 22, 2026; the elected councillors will then choose the mayor.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next elected Mayor of Arcis-sur-Aube.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Charles Hittler's commanding 91.5% implied probability in the Arcis-sur-Aube mayoral election stems primarily from his incumbency as the sitting mayor, bolstered by solid local polling and minimal organized opposition in this small French commune. Recent candidate announcements, including Antoine Renault-Zielinski's entry as a left-leaning challenger, have failed to shift trader consensus, reflecting Hittler's entrenched support amid stable voter registration and low drama. Annie Soucat trails at 6.5% as a distant third. Realistic challenges include a late anti-incumbent coalition, turnout surge favoring outsiders, or unforeseen scandal before the 2026 vote, though current evidence suggests limited upside for rivals.

events.description:

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. eventMetadata.context_description: Charles Hittler's commanding 91.5% implied probability in the Arcis-sur-Aube mayoral election stems primarily from his incumbency as the sitting mayor, bolstered by solid local polling and minimal organized opposition in this small French commune. Recent candidate announcements, including Antoine Renault-Zielinski's entry as a left-leaning challenger, have failed to shift trader consensus, reflecting Hittler's entrenched support amid stable voter registration and low drama. Annie Soucat trails at 6.5% as a distant third. Realistic challenges include a late anti-incumbent coalition, turnout surge favoring outsiders, or unforeseen scandal before the 2026 vote, though current evidence suggests limited upside for rivals.

question: Will Donald Trump visit Ireland in 2026?
description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits a listed country between the creation of this market and December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, the relevant market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Donald Trump's potential 2026 international visits remains fluid due to no official itinerary announced during the presidential transition, with implied probabilities favoring first-term destinations like Saudi Arabia, Israel, and NATO allies amid ongoing Middle East and Ukraine tensions. Recent cabinet picks, including Marco Rubio as Secretary of State nominee, emphasize continuity in Abraham Accords expansion and European security partnerships. Historical precedents show presidents averaging 10-15 foreign trips per year, often tied to summits; the US hosts G7 and G20 in 2026, potentially limiting outbound travel but enabling bilaterals. Early 2025 NATO meetings and diplomatic calls could catalyze shifts in trader consensus.

question: Unwell Winter Games: Winner
description:
This market will resolve to the team that wins the Unwell Winter Games.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by Unwell Winter Games rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the Unwell Winter Games competition is cancelled, postponed, or partially completed after June 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Unwell; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will there be a perfect NCAA bracket?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if anyone completes a perfect bracket for the 2026 Men's Basketball NCAA Tournament on any of the following websites: ESPN, NCAA.com, CBS Sports, Yahoo Fantasy, Fox Sports, Sports Illustrated, USA Today, Kalshi. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A perfect bracket is defined exclusively as a bracket that selects the correct outcome of every game during every round of the 2026 Men's Basketball NCAA Tournament. A perfect bracket only qualifies for a completed tournament. Perfect brackets for incomplete or partially completed tournaments will not be considered. No form of ‘second chance’ bracket will be considered. Only brackets that can be verified as having been submitted before the start of the first Round of 64 game will be considered.

If the 2026 Men's Basketball NCAA Tournament is cancelled, postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 Men's Basketball NCAA Tournament matchups have not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from ESPN, NCAA, CBS Sports, Yahoo Fantasy, Fox Sports, Sports Illustrated, USA Today, or Kalshi.

eventMetadata.context_description: No perfect NCAA men's basketball bracket has ever been completed in the tournament's 39-year history, with billions of entries across major platforms like ESPN and Yahoo yielding zero successes amid inevitable upsets and bracket-busting Cinderella runs. In the 2024 March Madness, early stunners—such as Oakland's defeat of Kentucky and NC State's rally past Marquette—eliminated all remaining perfect brackets by the second round, per official contest trackers, aligning with the 98.7% trader consensus on "No." Mathematical odds exceed 1 in 120 billion even under ideal conditions, though a freakishly chalk-heavy Final Four (all top seeds advancing flawlessly) remains a theoretical longshot that could revive faint hopes.
